import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut cur = 0
  mut has_digit = false
  mut neg = false
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i <= n
    mut b = -1
    if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        b = x
      when None
        b = -1
      end
    end
    let is_ws = b == 32 or b == 9 or b == -1
    if is_ws
      if has_digit
        if neg
          result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
        else
          result = list.push(result, cur)
        end
        cur = 0
        has_digit = false
        neg = false
      end
    else if b == 45
      neg = true
    else
      cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
      has_digit = true
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn emit_pairs(_ a: List<Int>, _ b: List<Int>) -> Unit
  let n = list.len(a)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    match list.get(a, i)
    when Some(x)
      match list.get(b, i)
      when Some(y)
        println("#{x}+#{y}=#{x + y}")
      when None
        ()
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(l1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(l2)
    emit_pairs(parse_ints(l1), parse_ints(l2))
  when None
    ()
  end
when None
  ()
end
